title: Pycnocephalum scapigerum
taxonomy: Pycnocephalum scapigerum is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Acanthaceae. It belongs to the genus Pycnocephalum. The classification further includes the kingdom Plantae, and the order Lamiales. This plant is native to Australia. The classification helps in understanding its evolutionary relationships with other plant species.

intro: Pycnocephalum scapigerum, commonly known as the 'Worm Tree', is a plant species native to Australia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its purported health benefits, which include reducing inflammation, improving cognitive function, and enhancing cardiovascular health. The bioactive constituents of Pycnocephalum scapigerum include phenylpropanoids and iridoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
